Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links 4OH-ICN with pathogen defense and fitness-promoting antibacterial defense.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: This summarizes the main conclusion: EPCOT3 acts as a CYP82C2 enhancer mediating WRKY33-dependent CYP82C2 expression and enabling 4OH-ICN biosynthesis and defense.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results include comparative promoter analysis, ChIP-PCR, transactivation assays in N. benthamiana and Arabidopsis protoplasts, and epigenetic signature analysis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Metabolite measurements for 4OH-ICN were performed in seedlings under pathogen challenge; defense assays were in leaves.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper tests the native CYP82C2 promoter containing EPCOT3 and shows this locus is sufficient for WRKY33 transactivation.

Omics_Data.reason: Comparative genomics/bioinformatics and published epigenomic datasets are explicitly used.
